applied_optimizations.name: fused_linear_sum_triton_kernel
applied_optimizations.description: Fused linear layer with sum operation using Triton kernel. Pre-compute weight_sum = sum_j W[j,k] and bias_sum = sum_j b[j], then compute output[i] = x[i] @ weight_sum + bias_sum. All subsequent operations (max, mean, logsumexp) on (batch_size, 1) tensor are identity operations and eliminated.

attempted_but_failed.name: initial_triton_kernel
attempted_but_failed.reason: Variable name typo: weight_sum_sum_ptr instead of weight_sum_ptr

attempted_but_failed.name: block_acc_store
attempted_but_failed.reason: Tried to store block tensor to scalar pointer
attempted_but_failed.error: ValueError: Value argument cannot be block type if pointer argument is not a block

top_bottlenecks: PyTorch eager mode materializes full (batch_size, out_features) tensor

notes: Key insight: After sum(dim=1, keepdim=True), tensor is (batch_size, 1), making max/mean/logsumexp along dim=1 identity operations
Optimization: output[i] = sum_j(linear(x)[i,j]) = x[i] @ weight_sum + bias_sum where weight_sum[k] = sum_j W[j,k]
Achieved 10.65x speedup (0.986ms -> 0.0926ms) with correct Triton kernel
BLOCK_SIZE = min(triton.next_power_of_2(in_features), 1024) works well for in_features=8192

global_notes: Key optimization: Fuse linear + sum, eliminate identity operations on (batch_size, 1) tensor
